stories.title: Game Recap — Rockies 5, Cubs 2 (June 16 at Wrigley)
stories.angle: PCA hit a leadoff HR but Cubs squandered the early lead. Cabrera exited early with a hand cramp. The bullpen couldn't hold it. The 28-46 Rockies took Game 2 5-2 at Wrigley. Cubs fall to 38-36. Losing to bad teams in a wild card race is unsustainable.

x_posts.label: Post 1A
x_posts.text:
PCA crushed a leadoff homer. That's the highlight.

Cabrera left with a hand cramp, the pen couldn't hold it, and the Rockies (28-46) beat the Cubs 5-2 at Wrigley.

38-36. Every loss to a bad team stings in a wild card race.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

stories.title: Bullpen Crisis — Palencia to IL, Maton Takes Closer Role
stories.angle: Closer Daniel Palencia placed on 15-day IL with right elbow inflammation (second IL stint of 2026). Season line: 2.70 ERA, 3 saves, 19 appearances. Gavin Hollowell recalled from Iowa. Phil Maton inherits the save role. Porter Hodge is already lost for the season; Harvey, Martin, Roberts also on IL.

x_posts.label: Post 2A
x_posts.text:
Daniel Palencia — 2.70 ERA, 3 saves, team-best closer — is now on the IL with right elbow inflammation.

Phil Maton inherits the save role. Porter Hodge is already gone for the year.

47 days until the deadline. The bullpen needs help too.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

stories.title: Trade Deadline — 47 Days, Rotation AND Bullpen Both Need Help
stories.angle: Palencia's IL adds a second position of need for Jed Hoyer. Horton (elbow surgery, season done), Steele (60-day IL), Taillon (hamstring IL) have stripped the rotation. Sandy Alcántara (Marlins) and Bailey Ober (Twins) named targets for starter help. Now the bullpen is depleted too. 47 days to August 3.

x_posts.label: Post 3A
x_posts.text:
The Cubs need a rotation arm before August 3.

Horton: out for the season. Steele: back in August at earliest. Taillon: still on the IL.

Sandy Alcántara. Bailey Ober. The names are there. 47 days.

#Cubs #MLB #NorthSiders

stories.title: Pete Crow-Armstrong — The All-Star Case That's Being Ignored
stories.angle: PCA is hitting .263/.341/.462 with 13 HR, 16 SB, and 3.9 WAR. He hit for the CYCLE on June 15 — the first cycle in all of MLB in 2026. Yet he has only 265,000 All-Star votes and ranks 14th among NL outfielders. The case has never been stronger; the recognition isn't there.

x_posts.label: Post 4A
x_posts.text:
Pete Crow-Armstrong: .263/.341/.462, 13 HR, 16 SB, 3.9 WAR. Hit for the CYCLE two nights ago — first in all of MLB this season.

265,000 All-Star votes. Ranked 14th among NL outfielders.

That's not a snub. That's a crime.

#Cubs #GoCubs #MLB

stories.title: Justin Steele — Throwing Program Starts Monday, Return in Early August
stories.angle: Justin Steele begins his throwing program June 22 (Monday). Return is 6+ weeks away at minimum — early August. His return could coincide with or follow the August 3 trade deadline, making him a potentially significant second-half factor if Hoyer doesn't add a starter.

x_posts.label: Post 5A
x_posts.text:
Justin Steele begins his throwing program Monday. Earliest return: early August.

Cutting it close to the trade deadline — but a healthy Steele changes the Cubs' outlook completely.

Still the most important development of the second half.

#Cubs #GoCubs #CubsBaseball

stories.title: Tonight's Game Preview — Cubs vs Rockies Game 3, 7:05 PM CT
stories.angle: Series tied 1-1. Cubs must win Game 3 to take the series at home vs a 28-46 opponent. Dropping two of three to the Rockies would be a damaging result in a wild card race. Starters TBD. Bounce-back game.

x_posts.label: Post 6A
x_posts.text:
Game 3. Series tied. Tonight at Wrigley, 7:05 PM CT.

The Cubs need to take this series. You don't let a 28-46 team walk out of Chicago with a split — not in a wild card race.

Bounce back starts now.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW
